name: 52 · Sample Swap — Live Twin (xArm 6, full pick/place cycle visualized)
description: Consolidates the pick-and-place cell (demo 40) with the live planning-scene twin (demo 51): a FULL multi-sample swap cycle on the SIMULATED xArm 6, watched end-to-end by ONE continuous 3D twin. A single LeaseScope holds one `xarm_fleet` runner; its body FORKS (`parallel_split`) into two branches that both ride the held runner: (A) a standalone `monitor_scene` ROS step that polls move_group's `/get_planning_scene` for the whole run and streams the live scene (arm joints + every sample box + whichever sample is riding the gripper) onto a Data channel the planning-scene twin renders on the `monitor_scene → twin_view` edge; (B) the sample work — reset → home → seed several sample boxes into the scene → a sequential Loop over `transfer_jobs` that, per job, resolves a UNIFIED location (rack slot OR instrument port) and dispatches a 37-pick or 38-place SubWorkflow. Because the grasped sample stays ATTACHED across jobs (one persistent move_group scene on the held runner), a pick-then-place pair genuinely carries a sample from the rack INTO the instrument port, and a later pick-from-port + place-to-rack returns it — each sample swapped in and out of the experiment, all visualized live by the single twin. The `monitor_scene` op is the new primitive that decouples scene streaming from any single motion (demo 51's stream was glued to one lift action), so ONE producer covers the whole run. The bulk stream rides the out-of-band JetStream datastream transport — the net sees only open + close. LIVE ONLY: requires `just dev xarm-up`; the full multi-sample run exceeds the 60s `mekhan test` cap — verify by launching a real instance and watching the twin + logs (the twin reflects the real move_group world, not a re-computed one).
